prompt.raw: Implement `fizzbuzz(n)`. Return "Fizz" if n is divisible by 3, "Buzz" if divisible by 5, "FizzBuzz" if divisible by both 3 and 5, otherwise the number as a string.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `reverseWords(s)`. Return the string with the order of space-separated words reversed, collapsing any runs of whitespace to a single space and trimming leading/trailing whitespace. Example: "  the  sky is " → "is sky the".
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `sumEven(nums)`. Return the sum of the even integers in the array. Empty array returns 0.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `twoSum(nums, target)`. Return the indices [i, j] (i < j) of the two numbers that add up to target. Exactly one solution exists. Return [] if none.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `isValid(s)`. Given a string of just the characters ()[]{}, return true if every bracket is closed by the matching type in the correct order. Empty string is valid.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `romanToInt(s)`. Convert a Roman numeral string (I,V,X,L,C,D,M) to its integer value, handling subtractive pairs (IV=4, IX=9, XL=40, XC=90, CD=400, CM=900).
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `groupAnagrams(words)`. Group the words that are anagrams of each other. Return an array of groups; within each group preserve the input order, and order the groups by the first appearance of any member in the input. Example: ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"] → [["eat","tea","ate"],["tan","nat"],["bat"]].
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `mergeIntervals(intervals)`. Given an array of [start, end] pairs, merge all overlapping intervals and return the result sorted by start. Touching intervals ([1,2] and [2,3]) merge.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `firstUnique(s)`. Return the first character in the string that appears exactly once. If there is none, return an empty string "".
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `lengthOfLongestSubstring(s)`. Return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `coinChange(coins, amount)`. Return the fewest number of coins needed to make up `amount`. Each coin may be used unlimited times. Return -1 if it cannot be made. amount 0 returns 0.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `spiralOrder(matrix)`. Return all elements of the m×n matrix in clockwise spiral order starting from the top-left.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `editDistance(a, b)`. Return the minimum number of single-character insertions, deletions, or substitutions to turn string a into string b (Levenshtein distance).
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `median(nums)`. Return the median of the array. For an even count, return the average of the two middle values (a number, possibly fractional). The input is NOT sorted. Empty array returns 0.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `roundHalfUp(x)`. Round to the nearest integer with ties (exactly .5) always going UP toward +Infinity, so roundHalfUp(2.5) = 3 and roundHalfUp(-2.5) = -2. Note: JS Math.round already does this — but verify the negative-tie behavior carefully.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `editDistance(a, b)`. Return the minimum number of single-character insertions, deletions, or substitutions to turn string a into string b (Levenshtein distance).
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `median(nums)`. Return the median of the array. For an even count, return the average of the two middle values (a number, possibly fractional). The input is NOT sorted. Empty array returns 0.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `roundHalfUp(x)`. Round to the nearest integer with ties (exactly .5) always going UP toward +Infinity, so roundHalfUp(2.5) = 3 and roundHalfUp(-2.5) = -2. Note: JS Math.round already does this — but verify the negative-tie behavior carefully.
prompt.label: coding

coding

prompt.raw: Implement `fizzbuzz(n)`. Return "Fizz" if n is divisible by 3, "Buzz" if divisible by 5, "FizzBuzz" if divisible by both 3 and 5, otherwise the number as a string.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `reverseWords(s)`. Return the string with the order of space-separated words reversed, collapsing any runs of whitespace to a single space and trimming leading/trailing whitespace. Example: "  the  sky is " → "is sky the".
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `sumEven(nums)`. Return the sum of the even integers in the array. Empty array returns 0.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `twoSum(nums, target)`. Return the indices [i, j] (i < j) of the two numbers that add up to target. Exactly one solution exists. Return [] if none.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `isValid(s)`. Given a string of just the characters ()[]{}, return true if every bracket is closed by the matching type in the correct order. Empty string is valid.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `romanToInt(s)`. Convert a Roman numeral string (I,V,X,L,C,D,M) to its integer value, handling subtractive pairs (IV=4, IX=9, XL=40, XC=90, CD=400, CM=900).
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `groupAnagrams(words)`. Group the words that are anagrams of each other. Return an array of groups; within each group preserve the input order, and order the groups by the first appearance of any member in the input. Example: ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"] → [["eat","tea","ate"],["tan","nat"],["bat"]].
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `mergeIntervals(intervals)`. Given an array of [start, end] pairs, merge all overlapping intervals and return the result sorted by start. Touching intervals ([1,2] and [2,3]) merge.
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `firstUnique(s)`. Return the first character in the string that appears exactly once. If there is none, return an empty string "".
prompt.label: coding

prompt.raw: Implement `lengthOfLongestSubstring(s)`. Return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
prompt.label: coding
